skill.name: clinicaltrials-database
skill.description: Search ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for trials by condition, intervention, location, status, phase, sponsor, or NCT ID.
skill.summary: Find, inspect, summarize, and export ClinicalTrials.gov trial records using API v2.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most are false positives from markdown examples, inline backticks, API names, and documentation tables. The real risks are legitimate network calls to ClinicalTrials.gov, optional bulk pagination, and documented CSV file export examples; no prompt injection, credential theft, obfuscation, or malicious command execution was found.

medium_findings.title: External API Requests to ClinicalTrials.gov
medium_findings.description: The helper script sends user-provided search terms, locations, sponsors, interventions, and NCT IDs to the public ClinicalTrials.gov API. This matches the skill purpose and is not malicious, but users should know that query terms leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network destination is a documented public government API and the requests align with the declared skill purpose. Confidence is high because the same script accepts user query parameters and performs outbound requests.

medium_findings.title: Bulk Pagination Can Generate Many Requests
medium_findings.description: The all-results helper paginates through matching studies and allows max_results to be omitted. This is legitimate for research exports, but broad searches can create excessive API traffic and should be rate-limited by the user.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The loop exits when pagination ends, so it is not an infinite loop. The missing default cap still creates a clear operational risk for large queries.

low_findings.title: HTTP Requests Lack Timeout in Helper Script
low_findings.description: The primary helper calls requests.get without a timeout. This is not malicious, but it can cause long waits or stuck processes when the external API or network is slow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The absence of a timeout is visible in both request calls. The impact is availability and reliability rather than direct compromise.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution indicators are documentation code fences, inline backticks, endpoint examples, and bash usage examples. No evidence found that the skill executes arbitrary shell commands or interpolates user input into a command runner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown examples, not executable skill logic. Review of the Python helper found no subprocess, os.system, eval, or shell command invocation.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic operations. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, password handling, or weak algorithm use in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files describe an API helper and markdown documentation. The scanner appears to have matched ordinary text and markdown tokens rather than cryptographic API usage.

low_findings.title: CSV Export Writes Local Files Only When User Runs Examples
low_findings.description: The filesystem write findings are examples that save API output to CSV files. This is expected export behavior, but users should choose filenames and directories deliberately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write calls are shown in documentation examples and use fixed local filenames. There is no evidence of hidden file modification or unauthorized filesystem traversal.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Network Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The script performs outbound HTTPS requests to ClinicalTrials.gov. This is necessary for the skill, but users should avoid sending confidential patient identifiers in search terms.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The requests are direct and easy to verify. The risk depends on the sensitivity of user-supplied query terms.

dangerous_patterns.title: Uncapped Bulk Retrieval Helper
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper can retrieve every page for broad queries when max_results is not set. This can increase API usage and processing time.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pagination loop is intentional and terminates on missing page tokens. It remains a meaningful operational risk for broad searches.

content.value_statement: Clinical trial data is hard to search consistently across conditions, interventions, sites, and statuses. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 and summarize relevant studies.
actual_capabilities: Search ClinicalTrials.gov studies by condition, intervention, location, sponsor, status, and NCT ID.
Retrieve detailed trial records from the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 studies endpoint.
Paginate through large result sets and optionally cap the number of studies returned.
Extract concise study summaries with NCT ID, title, status, phase, enrollment, and last update date.
Guide CSV export workflows for spreadsheet review or downstream analysis.
Explain API fields, response modules, status values, phase values, and common query patterns.
limitations: It depends on live ClinicalTrials.gov API availability and public rate limits.
It does not provide medical advice or determine patient eligibility by itself.
Search quality depends on accurate condition, intervention, location, and status terms.
Bulk retrieval can be slow for broad searches and should use explicit result limits.

use_cases.title: Find Recruiting Trials for a Care Team
use_cases.description: Search current recruiting studies by diagnosis, location, intervention, and status before reviewing official eligibility criteria.

use_cases.title: Track Trial Activity for a Research Group
use_cases.description: Monitor studies by sponsor, condition, phase, and update date to support literature review and portfolio analysis.

use_cases.title: Prepare Exportable Trial Lists
use_cases.description: Collect matching studies, summarize core fields, and prepare CSV-ready data for spreadsheets or internal reports.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Condition Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the clinicaltrials-database skill to find recruiting trials for type 2 diabetes. Show NCT IDs, titles, phases, locations, and last update dates.

prompt_templates.title: Intervention and Phase Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare trials for a treatment area
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ClinicalTrials.gov for Phase 3 trials involving pembrolizumab. Separate recruiting trials from active non-recruiting trials and summarize sponsor names.

prompt_templates.title: Location-Based Patient Matching
prompt_templates.prompt: Find recruiting lung cancer trials in California. Include trial status, nearest listed sites, contact availability, and a note to verify eligibility on ClinicalTrials.gov.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Portfolio Export Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 query plan for Alzheimer disease studies. Limit retrieval to 500 records, avoid sensitive patient data, and define CSV columns for analysis.

output_examples.input: Find recruiting breast cancer trials updated recently.
output: A ranked list of matching trials with NCT ID, title, recruiting status, phase, enrollment, and last update date.
A short note explaining which query filters were used.
A reminder to verify eligibility and site availability in the official record.

output_examples.input: Summarize one trial by NCT ID.
output: A concise study profile with title, sponsor, phase, status, enrollment, locations, eligibility highlights, and contact fields when present.
A clear distinction between official registry data and assistant interpretation.

output_examples.input: Prepare an export of active immunotherapy trials.
output: A suggested dataset structure with columns for NCT ID, condition, intervention, phase, sponsor, status, location, and update date.
Guidance to cap result count and respect ClinicalTrials.gov rate limits.

best_practices: Use precise medical terms and verify synonyms when searches return too few studies.
Set explicit page size and maximum result limits before running broad searches.
Review official eligibility criteria and site contacts before making clinical decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not enter patient names, record numbers, or other identifiers into public API query terms.
Do not treat trial matches as medical advice or confirmed eligibility determinations.
Do not run uncapped bulk searches when a focused query or result limit will answer the question.

faq.question: Does this skill require a ClinicalTrials.gov API key?
faq.answer: No. The ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 is public and does not require authentication.

faq.question: Can it find trials by NCT ID?
faq.answer: Yes. The helper can retrieve detailed study information for a specific NCT identifier.

faq.question: Can it determine whether a patient is eligible?
faq.answer: No. It can surface eligibility text, but clinicians and trial sites must verify eligibility.

faq.question: Does it send data outside my environment?
faq.answer: Yes. Search terms are sent to ClinicalTrials.gov when the helper script or API examples are used.

faq.question: Can it export results to CSV?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation shows CSV API responses and local file export examples.

faq.question: What should I do for large searches?
faq.answer: Use result caps, maximum page size carefully, and delays or backoff to respect rate limits.
